title: Harness, Scaffold, and the AI Agent Terms Worth Getting Right
content: We’re on a journey to advance and democratize artificial intelligence through open source and open science. This can be overwhelming for newcomers, and even for practitioners trying to keep up with the latest developments. After ICLR 2026, one of us ( @ariG23498 ) posted a question that captured this confusion well: "What do you mean by the terms 'harness' and 'scaffold' in the context of agents? I have heard a lot of explanations while I was at ICLR, but I could not understand why they did not converge to a single explanation." This glossary is our attempt to ground the terms that keep coming up without clear, consistent explanations. It is not meant to be a comprehensive dictionary of every term in the field. Instead, we focus on the concepts that are often mixed up, reused in different ways, or assumed to be obvious when they are not. Most of these terms come up whether you're building an agent, deploying one, or just using tools like Claude Code, Codex, or Hermes Agent. The last section covers concepts specific to training models, which is more relevant if you work on that side of things. Many of these terms don't have universally accepted definitions yet, and different frameworks use the same word differently. The goal here is not to enforce one correct vocabulary, but to provide a practical mental model that makes discussions easier to follow. Table of Contents Model Scaffolding Harness Agent Context Engineering Policy Tool Use Skills Sub-agents Training RL Environment Trainer Rollout Reward Learn More Model The model is the LLM: it takes text in and produces text out
scoreReasoning: 来源权威定义AI Agent核心术语，有助于未来讨论与开发决策，符合用户首要兴趣。
summary: The article clarifies confusing terminology in AI agent development. It defines “model” as the LLM, “scaffolding” as the instruction and tool setup guiding the model, and “harness” as the execution layer that runs the model and manages tool calls. An “agent” is the model plus harness; it acts in loops and can be trained via reinforcement learning. The piece also distinguishes context engineering, policy, tool use, skills, sub‑agents, and key training components (environment, trainer, rollout, reward).

title: Why AI Isn’t Killing SaaS Yet
content: The interview examines actual AI spending data, arguing that the fear of a “SaaSpocalypse” is exaggerated. Experts discuss how companies are buying and using AI tools, the rise of Anthropic over OpenAI in the Ramp AI Index, token-based pricing, productivity gains, and the unexpected resilience of legacy software firms. The conversation highlights realistic AI adoption trends rather than catastrophic disruption.
source: The a16z Show - content+a16zpodcast@a16z.com (Jack Farley, Max Wiethe, Ara Kharazian)

title: Bun 团队披露 Rust 重写审计结果：13000 余个 unsafe 块中七成可移除
content: Bun 团队于 5 月 21 日发布了一份关于其尚未发布的 Rust 重写版本的全面安全审计报告。这份由 AI 辅助生成的审计报告显示，Bun 的 Rust 代码库中共有 13,365 个 unsafe 语法节点，分布在 774 个文件、51 个子系统之中。经过逐项分类评估…

normalizedTitle: bun 团队披露 rust 重写审计结果 13000 余个 unsafe 块中七成可移除
scoreReasoning: 开源社区平台转述，非一手信息。Bun安全审计结果具体，但属技术细节，对整体工程效率影响有限。

title: 供应链攻击，正在蔓延，各位小心。逾 5500 个 GitHub 仓库在一场供应链攻击中遭感染
content: A supply‑chain attack has infected more than 5,500 GitHub repositories. Attackers increasingly control private keys, enabling stronger weapons and broader reach. The situation is escalating; users are urged to limit exposure by trimming workflows, disabling automatic updates, and tightening security. The spread is ongoing and likely to grow.
eventKey: 供应链攻击-正在蔓延-各位小心-5500-github-仓库在一场供应链攻击中遭感染

scoreReasoning: 社区安全警报，有实用警示，但属转述非一手，信源待核实。
summary: A supply‑chain attack has infected more than 5,500 GitHub repositories. Attackers increasingly control private keys, enabling stronger weapons and broader reach. The situation is escalating; users are urged to limit exposure by trimming workflows, disabling automatic updates, and tightening security. The spread is ongoing and likely to grow.

title: 做了个评论区模拟器 Kevlar-4u
content: 程序员 - @Ehzuiq - 做独立开发、内容创作最怕一句话没说好，隔天被敏感群体误读甚至冲上负面。毕竟我们自己看自己写的文案，往往有“知识的诅咒”和盲区。于是整了个新活，开源了一个内容风控/评论区模拟器：Kevlar 做独立开发、内容创作最怕一句话没说好，隔天被敏感群体误读甚至冲上负面。 毕竟我们自己看自己写的文案，往往有“知识的诅咒”和盲区。 于是整了个新活，开源了一个内容风控/评论区模拟器：Kevlar-4u 。 💡 它是干嘛的？ 把准备发的产品介绍、公告或贴文丢给它。它内置了 9 种经过严格脱敏的“基本盘画像”（如：理性严密男性视角、独立女性视角、务实职场人、硬核消费者等）。 抢在真网民开喷之前，让 AI 先在沙盒里对你进行一顿“模拟毒打”，提前找出措辞隐患或逻辑漏洞。 🛠️ 技术实现 基于 Multi-Agent 架构 + 本地 MCP (Model Context Protocol) 服务。 原生支持 Claude Desktop 、Codex 、WorkBuddy 等客户端，即插即用，确保内容隐私安全。 项目刚开源，初衷是帮大家在 Launch 产品时少踩雷。欢迎体验、提 Issue 或送个 Star ⭐️！ GitHub： [ https://github.com/9Churze/kevlar-4u ]
eventKey: 做了个评论区模拟器-kevlar-4u
normalizedTitle: 做了个评论区模拟器 kevlar-4u
scoreReasoning: V2EX开源工具帖，属论坛分享，工具实用但非重大生产力跃迁或版本更新。
